title: Find a visa application centre - Canada.ca
content:
## Find a visa application centre
Visa application centres (VACs) are private companies that have formal contracts with the Government of Canada. They
- are official locations to give your biometrics (fingerprints and a photo) You need to book an appointment to give your biometrics.
- You need to book an appointment to give your biometrics.
- securely send your passport to the IRCC office
- are located around the world
- speak local languages
If there’s no VAC in the country or territory you’re applying from, you can go to any other VAC .

## How to submit a passport to a VAC
Changes to visa and immigration services in Russia
As of January 28, 2026, VACs in Russia no longer accept passports or documents.
Choose a VAC outside of Russia that accepts documents when we ask you to submit your passport or travel document.
You can only submit your passport to a VAC after you get a request letter or email from us.
- Some VACs accept passports sent by mail or courier only .
- Check your VAC’s website for information on how to submit your passport or documents to us from outside Canada.
Examples of services that VACs offer include
- securely return your passport and the decision documents
- provide access to a computer to apply online
- provide application photo and photocopy services
- accept payment of immigration application fees
- use courier services to return passports and decision envelopes
- accept permanent resident travel document applications
Visit the VAC’s website before you go to find out what services are available.
VAC services and opening hours may change on short notice. Visit the VAC’s website for the most up-to-date information on available services before you go.
- VAC services and opening hours may change on short notice.
- Visit the VAC’s website for the most up-to-date information on available services before you go.

## Your information and privacy are protected
- VACs don’t play a role in the decision-making process and are expressly forbidden to provide you with any visa-related advice.
- All decisions on applications are made by Canadian migration officers at an IRCC office.
- VACs don’t represent the Government of Canada. VACs are managed by private companies or international organizations. They’re only authorized to provide specific services to applicants under the terms of a formal contract with IRCC.
- Protection of personal information is a primary consideration for the Government of Canada when selecting the VAC service provider. Each VAC must protect personal information. Any failure to comply with the terms of the formal contract between the Government of Canada (GC) and the service provider could result in the cancellation of the contract.
- If you suspect your privacy was breached, report a privacy concern. If you think a VAC breached your privacy when it collected or handled your personal information, contact the visa office that is processing your application. Don’t submit your application with your complaint . To file a complaint about how we handle your personal information or to ask a question about Canada’s Privacy Act, contact the Privacy Commissioner of Canada . Don’t submit your application with your complaint .
